content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

November 2, 1990

Maria McNamme Walp  
Post Office Box 11391  
Beverly Hills, CA 90213

RE: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. G-90-652

Dear Ms. Walp:

This is in response to your October 12, 1990, letter to the Commission suggesting that a statute be enacted to prevent candidates from paying outside voters to falsely register in their jurisdictions.

The Commission’s jurisdiction is limited to the provisions of the Political Reform Act¹ and the regulations promulgated under it. Voter registration is not within the Commission’s jurisdiction but is under the authority of the Secretary of State. Moreover, as I’m sure you are aware, neither the Act nor the regulations authorize the Commission to enact statutes. We would therefore recommend that you discuss your suggestion with your elected representatives.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin  
Acting General Counsel

By Steven R. Rhoads  
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:MM:dg

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
